facts.text: Harbour Heights lies on the northwest side of the Peace River, placing it in northern Charlotte County.
The community’s early history traces back to the Calusa people, who dominated southwest Florida from 500 BCE until the 18th century.
In the early 1900s, Harbour Heights saw phosphate mining, turpentine production, and timber harvesting before the 1920s land‑boom collapse left the area largely abandoned.
The area’s proximity to coastal waters makes it vulnerable to flooding, with historic sea‑level rise and storm surges causing multiple major events.
Harbour Heights boasts a high home‑ownership rate, with 83 % of its 1,939 housing units owner‑occupied and a median home value of $319,900.
Despite its affluent housing, about 13.6 % of residents live below the poverty line, higher than the surrounding Punta Gorda metro area rate of 8.7 %.
